name: Valid Accounts: Default Accounts
tactic: Defense Evasion, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Initial Access
description: Adversaries use default accounts built into systems and applications to gain access. In Azure/Entra ID environments, this includes exploitation of break-glass emergency access accounts (often named 'breakglass', 'emergency-admin', or 'admin@tenant.onmicrosoft.com'), default service accounts created by Azure services, or the built-in 'on-premises directory synchronization service account' used by Entra ID Connect. These accounts often have weak monitoring, exclusions from Conditional Access, and long-lived credentials configured at tenant creation.
iocs: SigninLogs with UserPrincipalName matching patterns: 'admin@*.onmicrosoft.com', 'breakglass*', 'emergency*', 'sync_*' from an unexpected IP
Break-glass account authentication outside documented emergency procedures — any use without a corresponding incident ticket is suspicious
SigninLogs ConditionalAccessStatus == 'notApplied' on a highly privileged account — indicates CA exclusion for emergency accounts
AuditLogs showing password reset of a default or sync account performed by a non-privileged user
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs showing Entra ID Connect sync account (SYNC_*) authenticating from an IP that is not the on-premises AD Connect server
SigninLogs for default service accounts with ResultType == 0 from a geolocation inconsistent with where the associated Azure service runs
Multiple failed ResultType 50126 against known default account names followed by success — targeted brute force
evasion_variants: Authenticate from the IP range historically associated with the organization's data center to mimic legitimate sync server behavior
Use the default account only for read operations initially to avoid triggering write-based alert rules
Leverage a default account that has been excluded from SIEM alerting as a 'known exclusion' in the detection configuration
Perform authentication during the expected sync window (e.g., every 30 minutes for AD Connect) to blend with scheduled service activity
Use client credentials grant (non-interactive) to authenticate the default service account, keeping it out of SigninLogs and into AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs where monitoring may be weaker
Rotate to the default account only after establishing a foothold with a regular user account — reduces detection surface during initial access
detection_kql_hints: SigninLogs | where UserPrincipalName matches regex @'(?i)(break.?glass|emergency|admin@.*\.onmicrosoft\.com|sync_[a-z0-9]+)' | project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, ResultType, ConditionalAccessStatus
SigninLogs | where ConditionalAccessStatus == 'notApplied' | where UserPrincipalName in~ (break_glass_accounts) | where ResultType == 0
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs | where UserPrincipalName startswith 'Sync_' | where IPAddress != adconnect_server_ip | where ResultType == 0
AuditLogs | where OperationName == 'Reset user password' | where TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName matches regex @'(?i)(break.?glass|emergency|sync_)'
SigninLogs | where UserPrincipalName matches regex @'(?i)(break.?glass|emergency)' | summarize count() by UserPrincipalName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1d) | where count_ > 0
